news.title: Icon opens early-phase research unit in San Antonio, plus satellite clinics to grow trial capabilities
news.snippet: Global contract research organization Icon has announced the opening of a new research facility in San Antonio, Texas, along with satellite clinics in Houston and Lawrence, Kansas, to expand its clini

news.title: With $1B Novo deal and diabetes ambitions undimmed, Lexicon is in ‘good shape to go crazy’
news.snippet: Lexicon CEO Michael Exton told Fierce how he set the company's development and financial pathway "on a very different trajectory."

news.title: Fosun pays $60M for option on AriBio’s phase 3 Alzheimer’s asset
news.snippet: Fosun Pharma has paid $60 million for an option on AriBio’s late-phase Alzheimer’s disease program, securing the chance to expand its rights to the asset in exchange for a further $80 million.

news.title: Valneva to lay off up to 15% of workforce in face of ‘adverse trend’ in travel vaccines
news.snippet: Valneva is planning to shrink its headcount by up to 15% as the French vaccine developer continues to seek ways to cut costs.

news.title: Humacyte cuts spending, staff as it seeks to boost sluggish sales for its off-the-shelf artery implant
news.snippet: Humacyte was riding high in December 2024 when it secured an FDA green light for a groundbreaking implant. Now, as it looks to boost sales of the device, it’s making sweeping cuts to keep up.

news.title: Medtronic to shutter Santa Rosa plant as it restructures cardiovascular unit
news.snippet: Medtronic will shutter its Santa Rosa facility that produces coronary stents and other heart-related products over the next two years as the device maker also looks to restructure its cardio business.

news.title: Takeda 0 for 4 against nausea and vomiting after axing another asset
news.snippet: Takeda has ended development of its nausea and vomiting drug candidate for strategic reasons, continuing its multi-year struggles in the therapeutic area.

news.title: AstraZeneca pens agentic AI licensing deal with Owkin
news.snippet: AstraZeneca is the latest Big Pharma company looking to tap into agentic artificial intelligence (AI), inking a three-year licensing deal with Owkin to leverage its autonomous “AI Scientist” platform.

news.title: Kyverna begins rolling submission for autoimmune CAR-T as FDA requests more natural history analysis
news.snippet: Kyverna Therapeutics has officially started a rolling submission to the FDA for miv-cel as the company conducts additional natural history analysis at the agency's request, keeping the candidate on tr

news.title: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary to resign, capping turbulent tenure
news.snippet: Marty Makary, M.D., is resigning as FDA commissioner Tuesday, ending a brief but tumultuous tenure marked by mounting criticism from all sides, according to reports.

news.title: Alphabet's AI biotech Isomorphic Labs bags $2.1B series B to fuel next-gen drug design model
news.snippet: If any proof was needed that big money is still available for AI drug development, look no further than the 10-figure series B hauled in by Isomorphic Labs.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker '26: Isomorphic's $2B series B; Cytospire's $83M for TCEs
news.snippet: The Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker records venture capital rounds of $50 million or more secured by biotechs.

news.title: Boehringer furthers inflammatory expansion with €407M biobucks bet on Immunitas asset
news.snippet: Boehringer Ingelheim has put up 407.5 million euros in biobucks to secure global rights to a first‑in‑class antibody Immunitas Therapeutics is developing for chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseas

news.title: AstraZeneca’s $800M bet undermined by immunogenicity in phase 3
news.snippet: Immunogenicity dragged on the efficacy of AstraZeneca’s rare endocrine disorder prospect in a phase 3 trial, causing the $800 million molecule’s response rate to fall short of the bar set by Ascendis

news.title: Charles River debuts AI diagnostic tool to cut down pathology timelines
news.snippet: Charles River is rolling out an artificial intelligence-backed digital pathology platform with a goal of trimming at least one week from standard pathology timelines and workflows.

news.title: BeOne's Venclexta challenger Beqalzi nets FDA approval as first BCL-2 for mantle cell lymphoma
news.snippet: BeOne Medicines has entered the BCL-2 arena, securing an FDA green light for Beqalzi that carves out a unique piece of territory ahead of a potential broader clash with market leader Venclexta.

news.title: As Trump arrives in China, Big Pharma CEOs are notably absent
news.snippet: As President Trump lands in Beijing, one major industry is missing from his business delegation—pharma.

news.title: Merck KGaA looks to M&A to bolster its 'rather slim' pipeline
news.snippet: Under new CEO Kai Beckmann, Merck KGaA is emphasizing M&A to stock its early- to mid-stage pharma pipeline, the company said in reporting its first quarter results.

news.title: Takeda, slimming down for 'new era,' plots 4,500 layoffs in latest restructuring drive
news.snippet: Takeda's multiyear slim-down is expected to hit about 4,500 roles during the 2026 fiscal year, resulting in about 170 billion Japanese yen ($1.07 billion) in restructuring costs and some 100 billion y

news.title: Novo, Lilly tout respective early response and weight loss maintenance data as GLP-1 rivalry intensifies
news.snippet: Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk are building cases for their incretin drugs, both new and old, as attractive weight loss maintenance options and a potentially speedier option to shed pounds, respectively.

news.title: BeOne Medicines’ Brukinsa TV ad 'Clarity' hit by FDA over 'misleading suggestions'
news.snippet: The FDA has issued an untitled letter to BeOne Medicines’ U.S. subsidiary over a TV commercial promoting its BTK blood cancer drug Brukinsa, saying the ad used “compelling and attention-grabbing visua

news.title: Haleon tackles sports injuries with latest soccer play
news.snippet: Haleon has completed a hat-trick of recent soccer initiatives, inking a pain management partnership with the organization responsible for protecting the health and safety of players in Europe.

news.title: Regulatory tracker: Pfizer nabs Hympavzi hemophilia expansion in Europe
news.snippet: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for

news.title: Valneva to lay off up to 15% of workforce in face of ‘adverse trend’ in travel vaccines
news.snippet: Valneva is planning to shrink its headcount by up to 15% as the French vaccine developer continues to seek ways to cut costs.

news.title: BioMarin consolidates staff at Amicus HQ after closing $4.8B deal for rare disease peer
news.snippet: Shortly after locking down its $4.8 billion deal for rare disease compatriot Amicus Therapeutics, BioMarin is consolidating the workforce tied to the acquired company’s headquarters in New Jersey

news.title: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary to resign, capping turbulent tenure
news.snippet: Marty Makary, M.D., is resigning as FDA commissioner Tuesday, ending a brief but tumultuous tenure marked by mounting criticism from all sides, according to reports.

news.title: Alkermes’ Lumryz hits phase 3 mark in another sleep disorder, fueling momentum from $2.4B Avadel acquisition
news.snippet: Alkermes has chalked up a quick clinical win from its $2.37 billion acquisition of Avadel, reporting Tuesday that a phase 3 study of the sodium oxybate drug Lumryz met all primary and key secondary en

news.title: As public vaccine criticism quiets, RFK Jr. keeps safety inquiries running in background: NYT
news.snippet: Amid apparent overtures from the Trump administration to curb public vaccine criticism ahead of the midterms this fall, the secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services is allegedly contin

news.title: Pfizer, Arvinas win $85M upfront in Rigel licensing pact for new breast cancer med Veppanu
news.snippet: The deal includes up to $320 million in future milestone payments plus tiered royalties on Veppanu sales, which will be split evenly between Arvinas and Pfizer.

news.title: Eli Lilly pauses Indian obesity awareness campaign after regulatory notices: report
news.snippet: Eli Lilly has paused an obesity awareness campaign in India in response to a regulatory notice regarding advertising rules for prescription medicines, Reuters reports.

news.title: Hantavirus risk remains low, CDC says, citing its ‘playbook’ response
news.snippet: Despite rising concern about the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, two CDC doctors say the risk to the public remains low.

news.title: STAT+: What the Trump administration wants in its next FDA leader
news.snippet: Leaders want the next FDA commissioner to be someone who can rebuild trust, focus on food policy, and drive drug-approval reforms.

news.title: U.S. overdose deaths fell again in 2025, but some worry about policy and drug supply changes
news.snippet: About 70,000 Americans died of drug overdoses last year — about 14% fewer than the previous year, according to preliminary government data.

news.title: The ‘velocity of obesity’ aims to show which nations are slowing an epidemic
news.snippet: A study finds the rise in U.S. obesity rates have hit a plateau in children but are still climbing for adults, and are still high compared to those in other…

news.title: STAT+: Gene therapy viruses linked to a boy’s tumor
news.snippet: The case for Makary as the WOAT, Cassidy's seat at risk ahead of midterms, and other biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA’s Makary resigning, a Lilly weight loss drug trial, and more
news.snippet: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary is resigning, and Kyle Diamantas, the agency's top food regulator, will step in as acting commissioner

news.title: STAT+: AI medical devices’ dirty FDA secret
news.snippet: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis newsletter: A look at the quirks of the most common FDA medical device approval pathway, sepsis algorithms, and more.

news.title: STAT+: Scientists link boy’s tumor to gene therapy viruses, in rare finding
news.snippet: Doctors have linked a boy's tumor to a gene therapy, but say the rare risks need to be weighed against the profound benefits such medicines can offer.

news.title: STAT+: Drug meant to make gene therapy safer may also make it less effective
news.snippet: Encoded Therapeutics gives a heads-up to other gene therapy company about the trade-offs with a drug meant to improve safety.

news.title: MPs call for sunbed advertising ban to help prevent skin cancer
news.snippet: A report by a cross-party group of MPs has found the majority of skin cancer cases are preventable.

news.title: Daily pill to help keep weight off after stopping obesity jabs
news.snippet: The tablet - orforglipron - is available in the US and could soon launch in the UK.
